features.evidence: I hooked it up to a 34-inch ultrawide monitor (3440x1440), the RTX 4080, Ultra 9 185H CPU, and 32GB RAM play almost every game at max settings with smooth FPS The Alienware software takes about 10 to 20 seconds to load when first opening it
Display is nice, all text is clear and colors are good I heard many people complaining about the brightness levels
Design is good imo Now, for value, you can definitely get better values out there, for example HP Omens gaming laptops, BUT, I love the design of this brand for a while and particularly this year
Keys feel great Keys are clear and not blinding like most RGB backlits
Battery life is quite good The battery health seems good, though this is something you'll keep plugged in all the time most likely

Why did you pick this product vs others?:Best performance I hooked it up to a 34-inch ultrawide monitor (3440x1440), the RTX 4080, Ultra 9 185H CPU, and 32GB RAM play almost every game at max settings with smooth FPS
2 usb 2 usb c, this is the only downside All ports in back

Now, for value, you can definitely get better values out there, for example HP Omens gaming laptops, BUT, I love the design of this brand for a while and particularly this year )So anyway, if you paid almost $2000 for this used laptop, go ahead and be angry, that's a poor value
I hooked it up to a 34-inch ultrawide monitor (3440x1440), the RTX 4080, Ultra 9 185H CPU, and 32GB RAM play almost every game at max settings with smooth FPS
Really fantastic gaming laptop in a small chassis
It handles anything I throw at it with ease, and the thermals stay well under 85*
The speakers and the build quality really blew me away in the x16
